heading: National Sheep Industry Improvement Center
label: 1627b National Sheep Industry Improvement Center

children.heading: Board
children.label: 1 Board
children.text: The term “Board” means the Board of Directors established under subsection (f).

children.heading: Center
children.label: 2 Center
children.text: The term “Center” means the National Sheep Industry Improvement Center established under subsection (b).

children.label: A
children.text: a public, private, or cooperative organization;

children.label: B
children.text: an association, including a corporation not operated for profit;

children.label: C
children.text: a federally recognized Indian Tribe; or

children.label: D
children.text: a public or quasi-public agency.

children.heading: Fund
children.label: 4 Fund
children.text: The term “Fund” means the National Sheep Industry Improvement Center Revolving Fund established under subsection (e).

children.heading: Intermediary
children.label: 5 Intermediary
children.text: The term “intermediary” means a financial institution receiving Center funds for establishing a revolving fund and relending to an eligible entity.

children.heading: Establishment of Center
children.label: b Establishment of Center
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a National Sheep Industry Improvement Center.

promote strategic development activities and collaborative efforts by private and State entities to maximize the impact of Federal assistance to strengthen and enhance production and marketing of sheep or goat products in the United States;
optimize the use of available human capital and resources within the sheep or goat industries;
provide assistance to meet the needs of the sheep or goat industry for infrastructure development, business development, production, resource development, and market and environmental research;

advance activities that empower and build the capacity of the United States sheep or goat industry to design unique responses to the special needs of the sheep or goat industries on both a regional and national basis; and
adopt flexible and innovative approaches to solving the long-term needs of the United States sheep or goat industry.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Center shall submit to the Secretary an annual strategic plan for the delivery of financial assistance provided by the Center.

children.label: A
children.text: goals, methods, and a benchmark for measuring the success of carrying out the plan and how the plan relates to the national and regional goals of the Center;

children.label: B
children.text: the amount and sources of Federal and non-Federal funds that are available for carrying out the plan;

children.label: C
children.text: funding priorities;

children.label: D
children.text: selection criteria for funding; and

children.label: E
children.text: a method of distributing funding.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established in the Treasury the National Sheep Industry Improvement Center Revolving Fund. The Fund shall be available to the Center, without fiscal year limitation, to carry out the authorized programs and activities of the Center under this section.

children.label: A
children.text: such amounts as may be appropriated, transferred, or otherwise made available to support programs and activities of the Center;

children.label: B
children.text: payments received from any source for products, services, or property furnished in connection with the activities of the Center;

children.label: C
children.text: fees and royalties collected by the Center from licensing or other arrangements relating to commercialization of products developed through projects funded, in whole or part, by grants, contracts, or cooperative agreements executed by the Center;

children.label: D
children.text: proceeds from the sale of assets, loans, and equity interests made in furtherance of the purposes of the Center;

children.label: E
children.text: donations or contributions accepted by the Center to support authorized programs and activities; and

children.label: F
children.text: any other funds acquired by the Center.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Center may use amounts in the Fund to make direct loans, loan guarantees, cooperative agreements, equity interests, investments, repayable grants, and grants to eligible entities, either directly or through an intermediary, in accordance with a strategic plan submitted under subsection (d).

children.heading: Continued existence
children.label: B Continued existence
children.text: The Center shall manage the Fund in a manner that ensures that sufficient amounts are available in the Fund to carry out subsection (c). The Fund is intended to furnish the initial capital for a revolving fund that will eventually be privatized for the purposes of assisting the United States sheep and goat industries.

children.heading: Diverse area
children.label: C Diverse area
children.text: The Center shall, to the maximum extent practicable, use the Fund to serve broad geographic areas and regions of diverse production.

children.label: i
children.text: all outstanding loan balances;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Fund balance;

children.label: iii
children.text: the outstanding balance to intermediaries; and

children.label: iv
children.text: the amount the Center paid for all equity interests.

children.heading: Influencing legislation
children.label: E Influencing legislation
children.text: None of the amounts in the Fund may be used to influence legislation.

children.heading: Accounting
children.label: F Accounting
children.text: To be eligible to receive amounts from the Fund, an entity must agree to account for the amounts using generally accepted accounting principles.

children.label: i
children.text: participate with Federal and State agencies in financing activities that are in accordance with a strategic plan submitted under subsection (d), including participation with several States in a regional effort;

children.label: ii
children.text: participate with other public and private funding sources in financing activities that are in accordance with the strategic plan, including participation in a regional effort;

children.label: iii
children.text: provide security for, or make principal or interest payments on, revenue or general obligation bonds issued by a State, if the proceeds from the sale of the bonds are deposited in the Fund;

children.label: iv
children.text: accrue interest;

children.label: v
children.text: guarantee or purchase insurance for local obligations to improve credit market access or reduce interest rates for a project that is in accordance with the strategic plan;

children.label: vi
children.text: sell assets, loans, and equity interests acquired in connection with the financing of projects funded by the Center; or

children.label: vii
children.text: purchase equity interests.

children.heading: Rate
children.label: A Rate
children.text: A loan from the Fund may be made at an interest rate that is below the market rate or may be interest free.

children.label: i
children.text: the useful life of the activity financed; or

children.label: ii
children.text: 40 years.

children.heading: Source of repayment
children.label: C Source of repayment
children.text: The Center may not make a loan from the Fund unless the recipient establishes an assured source of repayment.

children.heading: Proceeds
children.label: D Proceeds
children.text: All payments of principal and interest on a loan made from the Fund shall be deposited into the Fund.

children.heading: Maintenance of effort
children.label: 5 Maintenance of effort
children.text: The Center shall use the Fund only to supplement and not to supplant Federal, State, and private funds expended for rural development.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The management of the Center shall be vested in a Board of Directors.

children.label: A
children.text: be responsible for the general supervision of the Center;

children.label: B
children.text: review any contract, direct loan, loan guarantee, cooperative agreement, equity interest, investment, repayable grant, and grant to be made or entered into by the Center and any financial assistance provided to the Center;

children.label: C
children.text: make the final decision, by majority vote, on whether and how to provide assistance to an applicant; and

children.label: D
children.text: develop and establish a budget plan and a long-term operating plan to carry out the goals of the Center.

children.label: i
children.text: 4 members shall be active producers of sheep or goats in the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: 2 members shall have expertise in finance and management; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 1 member shall have expertise in lamb, wool, goat, or goat product marketing; and

children.label: i
children.text: 1 member shall be the Under Secretary of Agriculture for Rural Development; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 member shall be the Under Secretary of Agriculture for Research, Education, and Economics.

children.heading: Nominating body
children.label: A Nominating body
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint the voting members of the Board from nominations submitted by organizations described in subparagraph (B).

children.label: i
children.text: consists primarily of active sheep or goat producers in the United States; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has as the primary interest of the organization the production of sheep or goats in the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the term of office of a voting member of the Board shall be 3 years.

children.heading: Staggered initial terms
children.label: B Staggered initial terms
children.text: The initial voting members of the Board (other than the chairperson of the initially established Board) shall serve for staggered terms of 1, 2, and 3 years, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: C Reappointment
children.text: A voting member may be reappointed for not more than one additional term.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A vacancy on the Board shall be filled in the same manner as the original Board.

children.heading: Reappointment
children.label: B Reappointment
children.text: A voting member appointed to fill a vacancy for an unexpired term may be reappointed for one full term.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board shall select a chairperson from among the voting members of the Board.

children.heading: Term
children.label: B Term
children.text: The term of office of the chairperson shall be 2 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board shall meet not less than once each fiscal year at the call of the chairperson or at the request of the executive director appointed under subsection (g)(1).

children.heading: Location
children.label: B Location
children.text: The location of a meeting of the Board shall be established by the Board.

children.heading: Quorum
children.label: A Quorum
children.text: A quorum of the Board shall consist of a majority of the voting members.

children.heading: Majority vote
children.label: B Majority vote
children.text: A decision of the Board shall be made by a majority of the voting members of the Board.

children.label: i
children.text: the member;

children.label: ii
children.text: any spouse of the member;

children.label: iii
children.text: any child of the member;

children.label: iv
children.text: any partner of the member;

children.label: v
children.text: any organization in which the member is serving as an officer, director, trustee, partner, or employee; or

children.label: vi
children.text: any person with whom the member is negotiating or has any arrangement concerning prospective employment or with whom the member has a financial interest.

children.heading: Removal
children.label: B Removal
children.text: Any action by a member of the Board that violates subparagraph (A) shall be cause for removal from the Board.

children.heading: Validity of action
children.label: C Validity of action
children.text: An action by a member of the Board that violates subparagraph (A) shall not impair or otherwise affect the validity of any otherwise lawful action by the Board.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: If a member of the Board makes a full disclosure of an interest and, prior to any participation by the member, the Board determines, by majority vote, that the interest is too remote or too inconsequential to affect the integrity of any participation by the member, the member may participate in the matter relating to the interest, except as provided in subparagraph (E)(iii).

children.heading: Vote
children.label: ii Vote
children.text: A member that discloses an interest under clause (i) shall not vote on a determination of whether the member may participate in the matter relating to the interest.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary may vacate and remand to the Board for reconsideration any decision made pursuant to subsection (e)(3)(H) if the Secretary determines that there has been a violation of this paragraph or any conflict of interest provision of the bylaws of the Board with respect to the decision.

children.heading: Reasons
children.label: ii Reasons
children.text: In the case of any violation and remand of a funding decision to the Board under clause (i), the Secretary shall inform the Board of the reasons for the remand.

children.heading: Conflicted members not to vote on remanded decisions
children.label: iii Conflicted members not to vote on remanded decisions
children.text: If a decision with respect to a matter is remanded to the Board by reason of a conflict of interest faced by a Board member, the member may not participate in any subsequent decision with respect to the matter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A member of the Board shall not receive any compensation by reason of service on the Board.

children.heading: Expenses
children.label: B Expenses
children.text: A member of the Board shall be reimbursed for travel, subsistence, and other necessary expenses incurred by the member in the performance of a duty of the member.

children.heading: Bylaws
children.label: 12 Bylaws
children.text: The Board shall adopt, and may from time to time amend, any bylaw that is necessary for the proper management and functioning of the Center.

children.heading: Public hearings
children.label: 13 Public hearings
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Board shall hold public hearings on policy objectives of the program established under this section.April 4, 1996

children.heading: Organizational system
children.label: 14 Organizational system
children.text: The Board shall provide a system of organization to fix responsibility and promote efficiency in carrying out the functions of the Board.

children.heading: Use of Department of Agriculture
children.label: 15 Use of Department of Agriculture
children.text: The Board may, with the consent of the Secretary, utilize the facilities of and the services of employees of the Department of Agriculture, without cost to the Center.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Board shall appoint an executive director to be the chief executive officer of the Center.

children.heading: Tenure
children.label: B Tenure
children.text: The executive director shall serve at the pleasure of the Board.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: C Compensation
children.text: Compensation for the executive director shall be established by the Board.

children.heading: Other officers and employees
children.label: 2 Other officers and employees
children.text: The Board may select and appoint officers, attorneys, employees, and agents who shall be vested with such powers and duties as the Board may determine.

children.heading: Delegation
children.label: 3 Delegation
children.text: The Board may, by resolution, delegate to the chairperson, the executive director, or any other officer or employee any function, power, or duty of the Board other than voting on a grant, loan, contract, agreement, budget, or annual strategic plan.
State departments of agriculture;
Federal departments and agencies;
nonprofit development corporations;
colleges and universities;
banking and other credit-related agencies;
agriculture and agribusiness organizations; and
regional planning and development organizations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall review and monitor compliance by the Board and the Center with this section.

children.label: A
children.text: cease making deposits to the Fund;

children.label: B
children.text: suspend the authority of the Center to withdraw funds from the Fund; or

children.label: C
children.text: impose other appropriate sanctions, including recoupment of money improperly expended for purposes prohibited or not authorized by this Act and disqualification from receipt of financial assistance under this section.

children.heading: Rescission of sanctions
children.label: 3 Rescission of sanctions
children.text: The Secretary shall rescind sanctions imposed under paragraph (2) on a finding by the Secretary that there is no longer any failure by the Board or the Center to comply with this section or that the noncompliance will be promptly corrected.
